examples.english: He picked [fruits from] the date palms

senses.gloss: to pluck meat off a bone
to charge with a crime, to declare a criminal

examples.text:
a. 814, Abu Nuwas
جَفَانِي بِلَا جُرْمٍ إِلَيْهِ ٱجْتَرَمْتُهُ / وَخَلَّفَنِي نِضْوًا خَلِيًّا مِنَ الصَّبْرِ
jafānī bilā jurmin ʔilayhi jtaramtuhu / waḵallafanī niḍwan ḵaliyyan mina ṣ-ṣabri
examples.english: (please add an English translation of this quotation)

senses.gloss: a thing that incites anger, aggravates, or instigates ill-will
body, mass, bulk, volume of a body

examples.text: وَفِي ٱلْحَالِ، بَعْدَ ضِيقِ تِلْكَ ٱلْأَيَّامِ، تُظْلِمُ ٱلشَّمْسُ، وَٱلْقَمَرُ لَا يُضِيءُ، وَٱلنُّجُومُ تَسْقُطُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَاءِ، وَٱلْأَجْرَامُ ٱلسَّمَائِيَّةُ تَرْتَجُّ.
examples.english: the stars will fall from the sky and the heavenly bodies will be shaken.

examples.text: وَلَقَدْ تَرَكْنا الخَيْلَ فِي عَرَصَاتِهَا كَالطَّيْرِ فَوْقَ مَعَالِمِ الأَجْرَامِ
